description_long: This stone block features an ornate, high-relief carving of a heraldic shield adorned with three fleur-de-lis, flanked by stylized scrollwork and topped with a knight's helmet. The block is rectangular and relatively thick, with the detailed carving occupying the majority of the front face. The sides and back are rough and unfinished, indicating it was likely intended to be viewed only from the front. The surface is a pale, off-white stone, possibly limestone or marble, with subtle signs of chipping and wear along the edges. The carving is intricate, with deep grooves and raised elements that cast shadows, enhancing the three-dimensional effect. This type of block would typically be found as a decorative architectural element, such as part of a wall or gate in a historic urban setting.
description: A rectangular stone block with an ornate heraldic carving featuring a shield, fleur-de-lis, and a knight's helmet, in pale stone with rough sides and back.
description_view_0: The front view displays the detailed heraldic carving with shield, fleur-de-lis, scrollwork, and helmet, all in high relief.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the block's thickness and the pronounced depth of the carving, with a rough, unfinished stone surface.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a mostly flat, undecorated surface with some minor chipping and roughness, lacking any ornamentation.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the block's profile, showing the protruding carved elements and the rough, unpolished stone on the side.
attribute_location_context: historic urban gate or wall as a decorative architectural element
attribute_installation_method: mortared into a masonry wall or facade
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor chipping and edge wear are visible, with some surface smoothing likely from age and exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Elaborate carvings of a heraldic shield, fleur-de-lis, scrollwork, and a knight's helmet are present.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a continuous wall or decorative facade.
